explanation: The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV or PSV mode, and it's the end of the inspiratory phase, and an inspiratory pause is not required, and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is requested by the GUI, then eventually a lung recruitment procedure (RM) must be initiated. This captures the 'shall be possible to initiate' aspect as a liveness property, ensuring that if the conditions are met, the RM will occur.

This property states that globally, if the system is in either PCV or PSV mode, and the inspiratory phase has ended, and an inspiratory pause is not required (meaning it's not set by the GUI), and the GUI requires a Recruitment Maneuver (RMButton is true), then it must eventually be possible for the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure to be initiated.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in either PCV or PSV mode, the inspiratory phase has ended, an inspiratory pause is not required, and the GUI requests a Recruitment Maneuver (RM), then eventually (F) a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) will be active. This captures the requirement that the RM procedure becomes possible and is initiated when all specified conditions are met.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in either PCV or PSV mode, and the inspiratory phase has ended, and an inspiratory pause is not required, AND the GUI requires a Recruitment Maneuver (RMButton is active), then eventually the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) will be initiated. This captures the conditional possibility and eventual activation of the RM procedure.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is currently operating in either PCV or PSV mode (`PCVMode | PSVMode`), the inspiratory phase has ended (`inspiratoryPhaseEnd`), an inspiratory pause is not required (i.e., the inspiratory pause button is not pressed, `!inspiratoryPauseButton`), and a Recruitment Maneuver is requested via the GUI (`RMButton`), then a Recruitment Maneuver (`RM`) must eventually (`F`) be initiated. This captures the requirement that a Recruitment Maneuver becomes possible and eventually happens if initiated by the GUI under the specified conditions.

The property states that globally, if the system is operating in either PCV mode or PSV mode, and the inspiratory phase has ended, and an inspiratory pause is explicitly not required (as indicated by the GUI setting), and a Recruitment Maneuver is requested via the GUI, then in the very next state, the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must be initiated (i.e., become active).

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV or PSV mode, and it is the end of the inspiratory phase, and an inspiratory pause is not required, AND a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is requested by the GUI, then eventually a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) shall be initiated. This uses LTL template 49, where bool_exp1 represents the mode and phase conditions, bool_exp2 represents the GUI request for RM, and bool_exp3 represents the initiation of RM.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in either PCV or PSV mode AND it is the end of the inspiratory phase AND inspiratory pause is not required AND the GUI requires (sets) a Recruitment Maneuver, then the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) must be active. This directly captures the condition "it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure, termed Recruitment Maneuver (RM), if it is required by the GUI" by making the RM activation a consequence of the combined triggering conditions, indicating the system's capability to do so when all prerequisites and the GUI command are met.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode or PSV mode, and the inspiratory phase ends, and inspiratory pause is not required, AND the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is requested via the GUI (RMButton), then eventually (F) a lung recruitment procedure (RM) shall be initiated.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV mode or PSV mode, and the inspiratory phase ends, and an inspiratory pause is not required, AND the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) is requested via the GUI (RMButton), then eventually the Lung Recruitment Procedure (RM) will be active. This captures the 'shall be possible to initiate... if it is required by the GUI' aspect as a response to the GUI's command under specific conditions.
